title: Remote Work Adoption During the Pandemic
description: The COVID-19 pandemic led to a significant shift in work arrangements. By 2024, nearly 28% of workers were working remotely, up from around 2% in 2019. This change was driven by individual and firm-level responses to the recession, with some surveys indicating that up to 45% of workers switched to remote work. As a result, remote work became much more common than previously.
